channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Rubbish Dumped, 15th July
item.description:
These bags have been here a couple of weeks now.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tyler Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish Dumped, 15th July
item.description:
Black bin bag, looks like a duvet, left at the end of Tyler Street. Next to the wall of no.31

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tyler Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Full bin in park, 15th July
item.description:
Full bin in park
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Charlton Road, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox, 15th July
item.description:
Dead, decapitated fox, body and head. Seen today 13-07-26 lying on grass. Next to the flats, again.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bramhope Lane, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Leaning road signs, 15th July
item.description:
Two road signs outside Old Red Lion Pub (418 St John Street) have been hit and are leaning at dangerous angles.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 412-416, St. John Street, Islington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Illegally parked bikes, 15th July
item.description:
Bikes left on pavement, in doctors and residents bays.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bury Place, Bloomsbury, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Nw65px, 15th July
item.description:
Black bin bags and cardboard box dumped in street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Malvern Place, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Abandoned e-bikes causing trip hazard, 15th July
item.description:
Numerous and various branded e-bikes abandoned on both sides of the road causing a hazard for all especially those with disabilities.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bounds Green Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: O/S 16 St Julian's Road, 15th July
item.description:
Vomit and Human waste in a bag on the pavement. Bag has split and is a health hazard for pedestrians
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): St Julians Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown waste land mantained by the council, 15th July
item.description:
Recently we had a fire in the neighbourhood, which concerns me. This pice of land at the back of 25 Shirley Crescent is overgrown and needs to be cut back.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shirley Crescent, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Malvern place, 15th July
item.description:
Street cleaning has not been done

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Malvern Place, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown bushes blocking pavement 13 Highshore Road, 15th July
item.description:
Bushes growing from private property blocking pavement. 13 Highshore Road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 13, Highshore Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dog waste bags and litter being left where bin used to be. This is regular issue and health risk, 15th July
item.description:
Dog waste bags and litter being left where bin used to be. Since the litter bin on the corner has been removed by the council people are continuing to leave litter and waste in the area. Sometimes dog waste bags are attached to the post. This is regular issue and huge health risk. This issue has been continuing for a couple of months on the corner whereJasmine Grove and Oakfield Road meet.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 17, Oakfield Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Contaminated bins, 15th July
item.description:
The bins are overflowing bags all over the floor, the bin area looks like a hazard area, there are flies around the bin resident are complaining , the bins have not been collected for weeks due to the contamination. It is attracting foxes and rodents

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-100, Asylum Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fallen Tree Branch, 15th July
item.description:
Fallen tree branch hanging in the road just before the railway bridge
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maiden Lane, Barnes Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 25 + Voi bikes left on Brockley Way blocking street & pavement, 15th July
item.description:
There are at least 25 red Voi bikes on the left of the cemetery entrance in Brockley way blocking the pavement and road. On the right of the cemetery enterance there are also several Voi bikes strewn along the pavement! This has become and increasing issue over the past couple of weeks but this significant amount of bikes blocking the pavement and road is shocking! They need to be removed urgently!!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brockley Way, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped tray & wheel cover, 15th July
item.description:
Two items dumped at corner Hengist Rd ...moved out way of public.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hengist Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken bench, 15th July
item.description:
This bench has been broken for months now

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, London Road, White Oak, Swanley
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped chair, old wood and gas bottles dumped plus rubbish spread all over, 15th July
item.description:
Dumped chair, old wood and gas bottles dumped plus rubbish spread all over. Has not been cleaned for months. Needs urgent attention directly next to palace.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Corvette Square, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Paint spillage of public pavement outside property, 15th July
item.description:
On the evening of Friday, 10th July, paint appears to have been accidentally spilled on the pavement outside the property on Underhill Road, presumably by a passer-by. As the paint was still wet, pedestrians and cyclists have since walked and ridden through it, spreading the paint along the pavement and leaving an unsightly mess. Could this please be cleaned up as soon as possible, as it has become quite an eyesore? Thank you for your assistance.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Underhill Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
